questionHtml: Calculate the value of the rate constant, k, giving its units.
markschemeHtml:
k = ∆rate∆N2O5 ✔

«k = 0.75×10-3«mol dm-3 min-1»25×10-3«mol dm-3»= » 0.030 «min–1» ✔

min–1 ✔


M1 can be awarded from correct M2 if not explicitly stated.

Accept k = gradient.

Accept values in the range 0.028–0.032.

Award [3] for correct final answer.

meta.title: Calculate the value of the rate constant, k, giving its units.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 2. How much, how fast and how far?
Reactivity 2.2—How fast? The rate of chemical change

Reactivity 2.2.11—The rate constant, k, is temperature dependent and its units are determined from the overall order of the reaction. Solve problems involving the rate equation, including the units of k.
